title: aquilegia vulgaris var. fragrans
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris var. fragrans, commonly known as the fragrant columbine, is a variety of the Aquilegia vulgaris plant, belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. This plant is known for its delicate, bell-shaped flowers and is native to Europe and western Asia. The medicinal properties of Aquilegia vulgaris var. fragrans include its ability to act as an expectorant and anti-inflammatory, making it useful in treating respiratory issues and reducing pain and swelling. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to relieve coughs, colds, and flu symptoms, while also providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory health benefits.
